extraction.one_line_summary: FERRON Valentin attacked early from 6-man break with Bagioli present, exploited sprint hesitation as rivals marked Italian, held to line in Gap.
tactical_patterns: Powerful break formation caused peloton paralysis as multiple teams (Trek, UAE, Intermarché, FDJ) failed to organize unified chase, exhausting themselves.
Break exploited 4.6km descent entry as tipping point where chase became mathematically impossible despite tailwind-assisted speeds.
FERRON attacked at 1km exactly because rivals refused to chase marked sprinter BAGIOLI, creating prisoner's dilemma that broke sprint cooperation.

rider_signals.FERRON Valentin: positive: Recognized BAGIOLI's presence would cause tactical paralysis among break companions, timed solo launch to exploit this perfectly.
rider_signals.BAGIOLI Andrea: negative: Refused to chase FERRON's early move despite being strongest sprinter, lost both tactically and physically when finally responding.
rider_signals.VAN AERT Wout: neutral: Team explicitly preserving him for Alps rather than controlling break stage, signaling GC ambitions supersede stage hunting.
rider_signals.ROLLAND Pierre: positive: Extended polka dot lead while infiltrating powerful break, showing climbers can opportunistically target points on non-mountain stages.
